digest:
**Daily Macro Digest — 20 July 2026**

**Summary:** Risk appetite deteriorated over the last 24 hours. The VIX (a measure of expected S&P 500 volatility, often called the "fear index") jumped over 12%, an unusually large move, coinciding with a sharp 2.3% drop in the ASX materials basket (BHP, RIO, FMG, NST) — also flagged as unusual. Oil had the standout day, with Brent up 4.6% and WTI up 3.6%. Gold and silver both edged higher (+0.8% and +0.77%), while the US dollar index was essentially flat and the 10-year Treasury yield eased slightly. AUD/USD slipped 0.43%. Unfortunately, most news feeds returned access errors today, so specific catalysts behind these moves can't be confirmed from headlines — treat the connections below as plausible, not verified.

**Flagged assets:**
